title: Selenium WebDriver Basics
description: Build your first Selenium WebDriver automation test. Learn to initialize WebDriver, navigate to web pages, locate elements using various strategies, interact with page elements, and verify content. This is the foundation of web test automation.
instructions: Create a class `SeleniumTest` with a WebDriver field and setup/teardown methods
Implement `setUp()` method to initialize ChromeDriver with WebDriverManager
Implement `navigateToPage(String url)` to navigate to a given URL
Implement `getPageTitle()` to return the current page title
Implement `findElementById(String id)` to locate and return an element by ID
Implement `clickElement(WebElement element)` to click on a given element
Implement `getElementText(WebElement element)` to retrieve element text content
Implement `tearDown()` method to close the browser and quit the driver
learningObjectives: Understand WebDriver initialization and browser setup
Master element location strategies (ID, CSS, XPath)
Learn basic WebDriver operations (navigate, click, getText)
Practice proper test lifecycle with setUp and tearDown
Understand implicit waits and WebDriver management

testCases.name: Get page title returns correct value
Find element by ID locates element

hints.content: Use WebDriverManager.chromedriver().setup() to configure the driver, then initialize with 'driver = new ChromeDriver()'. Set implicit wait using driver.manage().timeouts().implicitlyWait(Duration.ofSeconds(10)).
Navigate using driver.get(url), get title with driver.getTitle(), and find elements with driver.findElement(By.id(id)). Element interactions use element.click() and element.getText().

Complete solution: In tearDown(), always check if driver is not null before calling driver.quit(). Use try-finally blocks in main to ensure tearDown() runs even if tests fail. This prevents orphaned browser processes.
